_doc: The 'session pinning logout trap'. Some servers don't issue a new session cookie on login - they associate the existing cookie with whichever user logged in last. So logging in as User B with cookies from User A's session can leave you authenticated as A (or some confused mix). Fix: explicit `clear_cookies: true` between identities, before the second login.

steps._comment: EXPLICIT identity-switch boundary. No request, just wipes the session jar. Without this, the next login may reuse User A's session cookie even though the body says 'username=carlos'.
steps.name: switch_identity
